title: Banksia fraseri
intro: Banksia fraseri, also known as the Fraser's Banksia, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Banksia that is native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Banksia fraseri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Banksia fraseri include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Banksia fraseri is classified as a species of plant in the family Proteaceae. It is a genus of evergreen trees and shrubs native to Australia. The Banksia genus is further divided into around 170 species, with Banksia fraseri being one of them. Banksia fraseri is a rare and endangered species found primarily in New South Wales. The classification of Banksia fraseri at the species level is within the Banksia serrata series.
